channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade blame over an attack on a boarding school in Russia’s Kursk region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Competing claims emerged over a deadly attack on a boarding school in Sudzha, a city in Russia’s Kursk region that has been under Ukrainian control for five months, with Ukraine and Russia accusing each other of carrying out the strike.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Colorado woman worked with stranger she met on a bus to kill boyfriend who questioned if she could land a job
item.description: A Colorado woman was convicted of murder after she and a stranger she met on a bus killed her boyfriend who was skeptical that she could land a job.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Punxsutawney Phil to emerge with his annual prediction about the end of winter
item.description: Punxsutawney Phil’s team of top-hatted associates will issue the woodchuck’s weather verdict as the sun rises.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Obama Center subcontractor files $40M discrimination lawsuit against engineering firm for overruns
item.description: A Chicago-based subcontractor is suing one of the firms involved in managing the construction of the Obama Presidential Center for $40 million, claiming racial discriminatory practices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beneficiaries under Social Security Fairness Act could be waiting 'more than one year' for adjusted payments, SSA says
item.description: The Social Security Administration, in an update to its website on Friday, said the department does not currently have the funding to execute the adjustments “in a timely manner and without negatively affecting day-to-day customer service.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Officials recover 42 remains from Potomac River as investigation into midair collision continues
item.description: Officials said on Saturday that they recovered 42 sets of remains amid the investigation into the Jan. 29 Potomac River midair collision, and plan on releasing more details in coming days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan priest has license revoked after imitating Musk's gesture at Pro-Life Summit
item.description: The priest, Calvin Robinson, attended the National Pro-Life Summit in Washington, D.C. last month. Toward the end of his remarks at the Jan. 25 event, he mimicked Musk’s salute, which made the rounds online.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Seven dead, 19 injured in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia
item.description: A person in a car was the seventh fatal victim of the fiery crash of an air ambulance onto a busy Philadelphia street, authorities said Saturday, as investigators sifted through burned cars, damaged homes and charred debris for clues to determine why the aircraft plummeted shortly after takeoff.
item.dc:creator: MATT ROURKE and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USAID website goes offline in Trump administration's 2-week-old freeze on foreign aid worldwide
item.description: USAID’s website has dropped offline without explanation in the Trump administration's two-week-old funding freeze of U.S. foreign aid and development funding.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Army releases name of 3rd soldier who died in helicopter and airliner crash
item.description: The Army has released the name of the third soldier who died Wednesday when an Army helicopter collided with an American Airlines jet near the D.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With home prices and mortgage rates high, many families find the American dream out of reach
item.description: The Petersen family’s two-bedroom apartment in northern California is starting to feel small. Four-year-old Jerrik's toy monster trucks are everywhere in the 1,100-square-foot unit in Campbell, just outside of San Jose.
item.dc:creator: R.J. RICO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Philadelphia mayor confirms 7 dead, including one on the ground, in medical transport jet crash
item.description: Six people on board a medical transport jet that crashed in Philadelphia were killed. That is according to Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, WGN 9

item.title: Author's story of coping after a wildfire resonates with community affected by latest LA-area fires
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — On a quiet summer evening in June 1990, Pico Iyer sat in his family home in Santa Barbara, California, when suddenly, he was surrounded by walls of flames five stories high.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate Democrats call for reinstatement of IGs fired by Trump
item.description: The lawmakers said that Congress did not receive the 30-day notice or the reason for the IG’s removals which took place at over a dozen agencies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: 525-pound bear found hiding under evacuated home after Southern California fire
item.description: Wildlife officials rescued a 525-pound bear found hiding underneath a home that was evacuated during the Eaton Fire.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KRON4

item.title: Man agrees to plead guilty for flying drone that damaged firefighting aircraft in LA wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The pilot of a drone that crashed into a firefighting plane, leaving a gaping hole and grounding the aircraft during the deadly Palisades Fire in Los Angeles, has agreed to plead guilty to a misdemeanor count of unsafely operating a drone, federal authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Air traffic controllers union responds to Trump's DEI attacks
item.description: The air traffic controllers union responded Friday to claims by President Trump this week that diversity programs contributed to the fatal crash in Washington Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia plane tragedy: Medical ambulance transporting pediatric patient crashes, explodes near homes
item.description: A Learjet 55 crashed Friday night in Philadelphia, sparking a massive explosion. The FAA said six people were on board the plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the NTSB, the agency investigating the DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people brought renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump refuses to say if he’s spoken to Putin; White House ‘talking’ about ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump on Friday wouldn’t say if he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since he began his second term but said there are ongoing discussions about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Rats! More rodents are infesting cities as scientists say warmer temperatures mean more rat babies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rat infestation in many world cities appears to be soaring, especially in Washington, and a new study blames warming temperatures, urbanization and other human actions.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina's ex-lieutenant governor dismisses lawsuit against CNN, has no plans to run again
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina's former Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson on Friday withdrew his defamation lawsuit against CNN that challenged a report about him making explicit posts on a pornography website’s message board over a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mother, daughter identified as victims in DC plane-helicopter collision
item.description: More details about the 67 people who died in the helicopter and plane crash outside of Washington D.C. are being shared. Several victims were Virginia residents from Loudon and Fairfax counties, ranging from parents to children.
item.dc:creator: Allison Williams, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Parents charged with neglect after 8-year-old girl dies from fentanyl poisoning
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- Two Indianapolis parents have been arrested and charged with neglect resulting in her death after their 8-year-old child died from acute fentanyl poisoning.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Special Report: Behind the stories we uncovered for Black History Month
item.description: Among the topics discussed is a deeper dive into our coverage of a surge in black-owned businesses from WHTM's Valerie Pritchett, WFLA's Deanne King delving into stories of everyday people working to fix systemic problems in black communities, and KSEE's Sydney Charles reporting on the role Buffalo
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Detectives believe missing woman's brother killed, dismembered her
item.description: A woman who went missing a year ago was declared dead by a Manatee County Probate judge, the Bradenton Police Department announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, NewsNation

item.title: 40+ bodies pulled from Potomac near Reagan National
item.description: More than 40 bodies have been recovered from the Potomac River after a deadly mid-air collision sent an Army Black Hawk helicopter and American Airlines plane diving into the icy water earlier this week, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Wichita airport sets up memorial for DC plane crash victims
item.description: Wichita Dwight D. Eisenhower Airport set up a memorial for the 64 victims lost on an American Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Zena Taher, NewsNation

item.title: Officials probe cause of DC plane crash
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 with 60 passengers and four crew hit an Army helicopter near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all aboard in the deadliest U.S. air crash in more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and HALLIE GOLDEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DC plane crash: Recovery continues, NTSB says investigation ongoing
item.description: Air crash investigations can take months, and federal investigators told reporters they would not speculate on the cause.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump talks so much that even his White House stenographers are struggling to keep up
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House stenographers have a problem. Donald Trump is talking so much, the people responsible for transcribing his public remarks are struggling to keep up with all the words.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Patel dodges, demurs as Democrats press him on controversial statements
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, wouldn’t acknowledge or sought to distance himself from a host of his past public statements as Democrats pressed him during his confirmation hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Man who killed five people after a neighbor complained about his gunfire gets life in prison
item.description: COLDSPRING, Texas (AP) — A man accused of killing five people in Texas in 2023 after a neighbor complained that he was keeping a baby awake by firing a gun in his yard late at night pleaded guilty Wednesday to murder charges and will serve a life sentence without the possibility of parole, a prosecu
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Things to know about the Trump administration order on miles per gallon for cars and pickups
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Hours after being sworn in as the new U.S. Secretary of Transportation, Sean Duffy took aim at the main way the federal government regulates miles per gallon for cars and pickup trucks — also a principal way that it regulates air pollution and addresses climate change.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican congressman suggests students get jobs to pay for lunches
item.description: Republican Rep. Ritch McCormick (R-Ga.) suggested that students should get jobs to pay for their lunches as the federal funding freeze proposed by President Trump threatens nutrition support for children across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
